name: pcs-promo-parser
description: Parse vendor promo decks (Milwaukee, DeWalt, Makita, Bosch, EGO, Flex, GearWrench, Crescent) into PCS Kit Builder Stage 1 outputs. Handles kit promos, NLP price drops, RSAs, and exclusions (POS Redemption, ARP, brick-and-mortar, etc.). v1.6.0: DeWalt-run correctness fixes — multi-paid bundle members and every included slot now emit an explicit 0.00 Price (never blank) with the bundle total on slot 1, so kb build-imports and decode-formula stop erroring / garbling on blank member prices; the bundle anchor (slot 1) is the main / highest-value tool regardless of which source row carried the price; the DeWalt SKU regex now admits bare corded DW#### SKUs (DW735 / DW7350 / DW735X) so they stop being held as sku-pattern-mismatch; DeWalt PMAPP = IN-STORE ONLY (and the deck footer ADVERTISING WINDOW: IN-STORE ONLY) is a per-SKU brick-and-mortar exclusion, and in-store / volume / spend-threshold BMSM with no online window routes to Non-Included instead of Other-Promotions (so it never becomes a Jira task); the repeated DeWalt confidentiality footer is ignored in new-product / brick-mortar triage; and the verification grep searches the whole multi-page promo range (not just the lead page) so continuation-page SKUs stop being falsely held image-only. v1.5.0: the kit outputs (Promo-List, RSA-Kits) gain a trailing `Page` column and the parser persists each rendered data page to `Promo Parsed Output/deck_pages/p###.png`, so the Jira task builder can attach the source deck image to each task and read it for POS/credit clues (the kb engine ignores the extra column). v1.4.0: robust + selective PDF ingestion — when the native PDF reader's rasteriser is missing (common in a Linux sandbox) it renders with PyMuPDF in-process (no poppler), and it skips cover/marketing/divider pages from the full-resolution vision read for big token savings with no accuracy loss; the Milwaukee SKU pattern now admits sub-brand forms (MX Fuel, letter-infix, bare models, Empire, Stiletto, apparel, layout codes) so legitimate SKUs stop being wrongly held to For-Review; ambiguous split battery slides ask the operator to confirm the tool-to-free-good pairing before holding. v1.3.0: a per-SKU + per-price verification gate runs before any output is written — every SKU/price is independently re-grounded in the deck (deterministic text grep + an independent adversarial verifier subagent + a vendor-regex check); anything ungroundable is HELD out of the Promo-List and surfaced in For-Review (Review Class `unverified`), so hallucinated SKUs/prices never reach NetSuite. v1.2.0: outputs are organized into `Parsed Decks/<Vendor>/<Vendor>-<QN>-<YYYY>-<MM-DD>/Promo Parsed Output/`; Buy-More-Save-More, e-rebate, and promo-code promos are now PARSED into a combined Other-Promotions.csv (no longer excluded); a conditional For-Review.xlsx collects low-confidence + missing-data pages with an in-chat table + link; empty outputs are no longer written, with the always-present Parser-Audit.csv as the run manifest. v1.1.0: split-slide per-group pairing, multiset `Get N` picks, Milwaukee on-slide pricing. v1.0.0: RSA-Kits/RSA-NLP outputs with credit amounts, Makita Needs-Pricing routing.
